ARTICLE 1 - KEY DEFINITIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1.1 "Index Account" - An account where interest credits are based 
    on the performance of a stock market index.

1.2 "Fixed Account" - An account earning a guaranteed fixed 
    interest rate.

1.3 "Cap Rate" - The maximum interest rate that can be credited 
    to an index account in any crediting period.

1.4 "Floor Rate" - The minimum interest rate (0%) that will be 
    credited, protecting against market losses.

1.5 "Participation Rate" - The percentage of index gain that is 
    credited to your account.

1.6 "Point-to-Point Method" - Interest crediting based on index 
    change from beginning to end of crediting period (annual).

     and any remaining surrender value is paid to you.

ARTICLE 11 - EXCLUSIONS AND LIMITATIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

     or reinstatement, we pay only premiums paid minus any 
     withdrawals and charges, without interest.

11.2 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
     misrepresentation for 2 years from issue date.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 12 - IMPORTANT DISCLOSURES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
12.1 This is NOT a securities product. Index participation does 
     not involve direct ownership of stocks or index funds.

12.2 Dividends from index components are NOT included in index 
     performance calculations.

12.3 Cap rates, participation rates, and fixed account rates may 
     change annually but will never go below guaranteed minimums.

12.4 Past index performance does not guarantee future results.

12.5 Zero is your hero - you cannot lose money due to negative 
     index performance, but you also don't participate in 
     dividends.

results.question: What happens if the index declines in a given year?

results.llm_answer: If the index declines in a given year, the account is protected by the floor, and no interest is credited—resulting in 0% interest for that year. The principal is not lost due to market decline.

ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)
    
    If index declined 5%:
    • Floor protects at 0%
    • Interest credited: 0% (no loss)

3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The 
    worst case is 0% interest for that year.

results.question: Under Option 1, if the account value grows over time, what happens to the cost of insurance charge?
results.expected_answer: The COI charge decreases, because it is calculated as (Death Benefit minus Account Value) times the COI rate; as the account value grows, the net amount at risk decreases, reducing the COI.
results.evidence: 6.1 OPTION 1 - Level Death Benefit (CURRENT): Death Benefit = $500,000 (specified amount) / 5.1 Cost of Insurance (COI): Deducted monthly based on: (Death Benefit - Account Value) × Monthly COI Rate ÷ 1,000
results.llm_answer: Under Option 1, as the account value grows, the cost of insurance charge decreases because the difference between the fixed death benefit and the account value becomes smaller.

results.question: If the S&P 500 index gains 8% in a year, what interest is credited to the S&P 500 Index Account, and is the cap invoked?
results.expected_answer: 8% interest is credited. The cap of 10.5% is not invoked because the gain is below the cap rate.
results.evidence: A) S&P 500 Index Account (Annual Point-to-Point) / Current Cap: 10.5% | Participation Rate: 100% / Floor: 0% / 3.1 Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate (subject to Cap and Floor)
results.llm_answer: The interest credited to the S&P 500 Index Account is 8%, and the cap is not invoked because the index gain of 8% is below the 10.5% cap.

results.llm_answer: No, coverage will not continue if the No Lapse Guarantee Rider is not elected and the account value drops to zero. The policy will lapse if premiums are not paid and the account value is insufficient to cover monthly deductions.

ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)
    
    If index declined 5%:
    • Floor protects at 0%
    • Interest credited: 0% (no loss)

3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The 
    worst case is 0% interest for that year.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1.2 "Fixed Account" - An account earning a guaranteed fixed 
    interest rate.

1.3 "Cap Rate" - The maximum interest rate that can be credited 
    to an index account in any crediting period.

1.4 "Floor Rate" - The minimum interest rate (0%) that will be 
    credited, protecting against market losses.

1.5 "Participation Rate" - The percentage of index gain that is 
    credited to your account.

1.6 "Point-to-Point Method" - Interest crediting based on index 
    change from beginning to end of crediting period (annual).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 2 - INDEXING OPTIONS AND ALLOCATION

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 5 - MONTHLY POLICY CHARGES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5.1 Cost of Insurance (COI):
    Deducted monthly based on:
    (Death Benefit - Account Value) × Monthly COI Rate ÷ 1,000

5.2 Administrative Charge: $12 per month

5.3 Total charges are deducted from account value on the 3rd of 
    each month.

5.4 COI rates are guaranteed not to exceed rates shown in the 
    policy schedule.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
6.1 OPTION 1 - Level Death Benefit (CURRENT)
    Death Benefit = $500,000 (specified amount)
    Lower ongoing charges

6.2 OPTION 2 - Increasing Death Benefit
    Death Benefit = Specified Amount + Account Value
    Higher ongoing charges, benefit grows with account

6.3 You may change options on any anniversary with 60 days notice.
    Evidence of insurability required to increase coverage.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 1 - KEY DEFINITIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1.1 "Index Account" - An account where interest credits are based 
    on the performance of a stock market index.

1.2 "Fixed Account" - An account earning a guaranteed fixed 
    interest rate.

1.3 "Cap Rate" - The maximum interest rate that can be credited 
    to an index account in any crediting period.

1.4 "Floor Rate" - The minimum interest rate (0%) that will be 
    credited, protecting against market losses.

1.5 "Participation Rate" - The percentage of index gain that is 
    credited to your account.

1.6 "Point-to-Point Method" - Interest crediting based on index 
    change from beginning to end of crediting period (annual).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

════════════════════════════════════════════════

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)
    
    If index declined 5%:
    • Floor protects at 0%
    • Interest credited: 0% (no loss)

3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The 
    worst case is 0% interest for that year.

ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)

ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)
    
    If index declined 5%:
    • Floor protects at 0%
    • Interest credited: 0% (no loss)

3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The 
    worst case is 0% interest for that year.

2.2 FIXED ACCOUNT:
    Guaranteed interest rate: 3.0% annually

2.3 CURRENT ALLOCATION:
    S&P 500 Index: 50%
    NASDAQ-100 Index: 30%
    Fixed Account: 20%

2.4 You may change allocations on any policy anniversary at no 
    charge. First 4 reallocations per year are free; $25 fee 
    thereafter.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)
    
    If index declined 5%:
    • Floor protects at 0%
    • Interest credited: 0% (no loss)

3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The 
    worst case is 0% interest for that year.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 4 - PREMIUM FLEXIBILITY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
4.1 You may pay premiums at any time in any amount between:
    Minimum: $100 per payment
    Maximum: $50,000 per year (tax qualification limit)

4.2 PLANNED PREMIUM: $6,000 annually is recommended to maintain 
    coverage to age 100 based on current assumptions.

4.3 You may skip premium payments as long as account value is 
    sufficient to cover monthly deductions.

4.4 PREMIUM LOAD: 3.5% of each premium payment is deducted for

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5.1 Cost of Insurance (COI):
    Deducted monthly based on:
    (Death Benefit - Account Value) × Monthly COI Rate ÷ 1,000

5.2 Administrative Charge: $12 per month

id,	content
1,	B) NASDAQ-100 Index Account (Annual Point-to-Point)
       Current Cap: 9.0% | Participation Rate: 100%
       Floor: 0% | Crediting Period: 1 year
    
    C) Global Diversified Index Account
       Current Cap: 8.5% | Participation Rate: 100%
       Floor: 0% | Crediting Period: 1 year

2.2 FIXED ACCOUNT:
    Guaranteed interest rate: 3.0% annually
    No cap, no floor variability

2.3 CURRENT ALLOCATION:
    S&P 500 Index: 50%
    NASDAQ-100 Index: 30%
    Fixed Account: 20%

2.4 You may change allocations on any policy anniversary at no 
    charge. First 4 reallocations per year are free; $25 fee 
    thereafter.

2.5 GUARANTEED RATES: While current caps and participation rates 
    are shown above, we guarantee:
    • Caps will never be less than 4%
    • Participation rates will never be less than 25%
    • Fixed account will never be less than 2%

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)
    
    If index declined 5%:
    • Floor protects at 0%
    • Interest credited: 0% (no loss)

3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The 
    worst case is 0% interest for that year.

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

ARTICLE 5 - MONTHLY POLICY CHARGES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5.1 Cost of Insurance (COI):
    Deducted monthly based on:
    (Death Benefit - Account Value) × Monthly COI Rate ÷ 1,000

5.2 Administrative Charge: $12 per month

5.3 Total charges are deducted from account value on the 3rd of 
    each month.

5.4 COI rates are guaranteed not to exceed rates shown in the 
    policy schedule.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 6 - DEATH BENEFIT OPTIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
6.1 OPTION 1 - Level Death Benefit (CURRENT)
    Death Benefit = $500,000 (specified amount)
    Lower ongoing charges

6.2 OPTION 2 - Increasing Death Benefit
    Death Benefit = Specified Amount + Account Value
    Higher ongoing charges, benefit grows with account

6.3 You may change options on any anniversary with 60 days notice.
    Evidence of insurability required to increase coverage.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 7 - LOANS AND WITHDRAWALS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
7.1 POLICY LOANS:
    • Available after 1 year
    • Maximum: 90% of surrender value
    • Loan interest: 4% annually (fixed loan rate)
    • Loaned amounts earn fixed account rate (3%)
    • Net cost: 1% annually

7.2 PARTIAL WITHDRAWALS:
    • Minimum: $500
    • Maximum: Account value minus surrender charge
    • First 10 withdrawals free; $50 fee thereafter
    • Red

ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)
    
    If index declined 5%:
    • Floor protects at 0%
    • Interest credited: 0% (no loss)

3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The 
    worst case is 0% interest for that year.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 3 - HOW INDEX CREDITING WORKS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 CREDITING CALCULATION:
    On each policy anniversary, we calculate:
    
    Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) 
                     ÷ Starting Index Value
    
    Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate
                       (subject to Cap and Floor)

3.2 EXAMPLE:
    Assume S&P 500 Index:
    • Started at 4,000
    • Ended at 4,600
    • Gain: 15%
    • With 100% participation and 10.5% cap
    • Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)
    
    If index declined 5%:
    • Floor protects at 0%
    • Interest credited: 0% (no loss)

3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The 
    worst case is 0% interest for that year.

    Year 3: 8%  | Year 6: 5% | Year 9: 2% | Year 12+: 0%

8.2 Surrender charge is calculated as a percentage of premiums 
    paid in the first 10 years.

8.3 Cash Surrender Value = Account Value - Surrender Charge - 
    Outstanding Loans

8.4 FREE PARTIAL WITHDRAWAL: You may withdraw up to 10% of 
    account value each year without surrender charge after Year 1.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 9 - NO LAPSE GUARANTEE RIDER (OPTIONAL - NOT SELECTED)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Available as optional rider: Guarantees coverage will not lapse 
even if account value drops to zero, as long as minimum guaranteed 
premiums are paid. Not currently elected on this policy.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 10 - POLICY LAPSE AND GRACE PERIOD
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
10.1 If account value is insufficient to pay monthly deductions, 
     we will notify you at least 30 days before lapse.

10.2 Grace period: 61 days to pay sufficient premium to bring 
     policy back in force.

10.3 If premium not received within grace period, policy lapses 
     and any remaining surrender value is paid to you.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 11 - EXCLUSIONS AND LIMITATIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
11.1 SUICIDE: If insured commits suicide within 2 years of issue 
     or reinstatement, we pay only premiums paid minus any 
     withdrawals and charges, without interest.

11.2 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
     misrepresentation for 2 years from issue date.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ARTICLE 12 - IMPORTANT DISCLOSURES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
12.1 This is NOT a securities product. Index participation does 
     not involve direct ownership of stocks or index funds.

12.2 Dividends from index components are NOT included in index 
     performance calculations.

12.3 Cap rates, participation rates, and fixed account rates may 
     change annually but will never go below guaranteed minimums.

12.4 Past index performance does not guarantee future results.

12.5 Zero is your hero - you cannot lose money due to negative 
     index performance, but you also don't participate in 
     dividends.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
